title: Bitcoin Holds $64K as Crypto Mixed; SpaceX Stake Valued at $6.83B
excerpt: Bitcoin stabilizes at $64,483 with 1.17% gain; KHC values SpaceX stake at $6.83B; Solana and BNB outpace Ethereum.
content: # MoneyTailors Daily Market Brief **Sunday, 14 June 2026** --- ## HOOK HEADLINE Bitcoin Holds $64K as Crypto Mixed; SpaceX Stake Valued at $6.83B --- ## MARKET OVERVIEW Cryptocurrency markets show modest gains Sunday as Bitcoin stabilizes near $64,500 following a 1.17% 24-hour advance. Ethereum trades with marginal upside while smaller-cap assets including Solana and BNB post stronger percentage moves. In traditional markets, attention turns to private equity valuations, with a significant space-tech holding commanding fresh price discovery. --- ## CRYPTO MARKETS Bitcoin trades at $64,483, up 1.17% over the past 24 hours as sellers show restraint ahead of the new trading week. Ethereum follows with a more modest 0.14% gain to $1,674.77, reflecting consolidation in the second-largest cryptocurrency by market cap. Solana and BNB outpace the broader market, posting 1.42% and 1.20% respective gains to $68.33 and $611.47, signaling selective demand for mid-cap tokens. --- ## EQUITIES: PRIVATE EQUITY SNAPSHOT KHC has valued its SpaceX stake at approximately $6.83 billion according to recent fair-value assessment, according to Argaam. The valuation reflects the continued investor appetite for exposure to commercial space infrastructure, even as traditional equity markets navigate macro headwinds. This marks a material position within the broader alternative assets landscape. --- ## FOREX, COMMODITIES & GULF MARKETS No verified headlines on currency pairs, crude oil, gold, or regional bourses including Tadawul and PSX were available for Sunday's brief. Traders should monitor Monday's Asian market open for positioning shifts and any macro commentary from central banks or commodity producers. --- ## WHAT TO WATCH - **Crypto Weekly Momentum**: Track whether BTC sustains above $64K as institutional traders position for the week ahead. - **Equity Earnings Season**: Monitor any updates on mega-cap tech or defense-contractor exposure tied to space-economy holdings. ---

title: Bitcoin Edges Higher to $64.5K as Crypto Steadies; Logistics Costs Rising
excerpt: Bitcoin gains 1.24% to $64,527 as crypto steadies. FedEx rate increases pressure corporate supply chains in 2026.
content:
# MoneyTailors Daily Market Brief **Sunday, 14 June 2026** --- ## HOOK HEADLINE Bitcoin Edges Higher to $64.5K as Crypto Steadies; Logistics Costs Rising ---

Cryptocurrency markets show modest gains this Sunday, with Bitcoin climbing 1.24% to $64,527 and Ethereum holding near $1,676. The broader digital asset space reflects cautious positioning ahead of the week. In traditional markets, logistics operators report rising parcel shipping costs as major carriers implement 2026 rate increases, signaling inflationary pressure in supply chain operations.

## Cryptocurrency: Modest Sunday Gains Across Major Coins Bitcoin trades at $64,527, up 1.24% over the past 24 hours, while Ethereum edges forward 0.14% to $1,675.70. Solana outperforms with a 1.51% gain to $68.40, and BNB rises 1.21% to $611.32. The moves reflect steady, if unremarkable, weekend momentum as major cryptocurrencies consolidate recent levels without directional conviction. ## Macro: FedEx Rate Increases Put Pressure on Corporate Cost Structures Freight and logistics costs are rising in 2026 as FedEx implements rate increases affecting parcel shipping nationwide. Broussard Logistics reports that companies are now conducting detailed audits of parcel expenses to manage the impact of these carrier rate adjustments. Broussard Logistics notes that rising transportation costs are a material concern for supply chain managers seeking to protect margins amid inflationary pressure in logistics. --- **What to Watch:** • Monitor corporate earnings guidance for supply chain cost commentary as companies report Q2 results this week • Track broader inflation data releases to assess whether transportation cost increases will feed into consumer pricing • Watch for additional carrier announcements regarding 2026 rate adjustments that could compound logistics expense pressures ---
